serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Montepulciano d'Abruzzo DOC red wine is approximately 150 ml (150 g), equivalent to one standard wine glass as commonly served in restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Wine, table, red — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174835)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for red table wine used as the closest approved source. Montepulciano d'Abruzzo DOC is a dry Italian red wine with typical alcohol content of 12–13% ABV, consistent with the USDA red table wine profile. Values are representative of the wine category; specific vintage and producer data are not available in approved nutrient databases.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care, American Diabetes Association (peer-reviewed journal); University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: Dry red wine contains negligible fermentable carbohydrates (approximately 2.6 g per 100 ml, predominantly non-glycemic residual sugars and alcohol). The University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature consistently classify dry wine as having a negligible glycemic response. GI is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) as this beverage contains no meaningful glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category is 'low' per convention. Glycemic load per 150 g serving is 0.0 given the effectively zero glycemic carbohydrate content.

hypertension.guidance: Moderate alcohol consumption from red wine may acutely lower blood pressure via vasodilation, but regular or excessive intake raises systolic blood pressure; individuals with hypertension should limit intake to no more than one standard drink per day and consult their physician.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Alcohol-induced hypoglycaemia risk and caloric contribution
type2_diabetes.guidance: Dry red wine has a negligible glycemic index and minimal residual sugar, but alcohol can mask hypoglycaemia symptoms and impair gluconeogenesis; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume no more than one standard drink with food and monitor blood glucose closely.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Alcohol content, polyphenols, and cardiovascular risk-benefit balance
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Montepulciano d'Abruzzo red wine contains polyphenols including resveratrol that may confer modest cardioprotective effects at low intake, but the net cardiovascular risk from alcohol consumption increases with quantity; current evidence does not support recommending wine for cardiovascular protection, and individuals with established cardiovascular disease should follow clinician guidance on alcohol limits.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Montepulciano d'Abruzzo DOC is a Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) red wine produced in the Abruzzo region of central Italy, made primarily from the Montepulciano grape variety. The 'Rosso del Duca' label by Villa Medoro is a commercially available expression of this appellation, with the 2016 vintage representing a typical dry Italian red wine. The wine presents a dark ruby red colour with aromas of cherry, sour cherry, cocoa, and coffee, reflecting the characteristic profile of the Montepulciano grape. It is produced by alcoholic fermentation of red grape must followed by ageing, typically in oak or stainless steel. Nutritionally, dry red wine provides approximately 85 kcal per 100 ml, with negligible protein (0.07 g), no fat, and approximately 2.6 g of carbohydrate per 100 ml, predominantly residual sugars. It contains sulphites as a preservative and is not considered halal. The glycemic index is effectively zero due to the absence of glycemic carbohydrates, though alcohol itself contributes calories and can affect blood glucose regulation. Clinically, moderate red wine consumption is associated with polyphenol intake including resveratrol, but alcohol carries risks for individuals with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease. A standard serving is 150 ml. This wine is served at Ricciotti - The Riverwalk in Singapore and is priced at approximately SGD 102.59 per bottle.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy red table wine (FDC ID 174835), which is the closest approved source for a dry Italian red wine. No specific Montepulciano d'Abruzzo DOC entry exists in any approved primary nutrient database. The USDA red wine profile is representative of this wine style. GI is estimated as effectively zero based on peer-reviewed literature and University of Sydney GI Database classification of dry wines. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact match exists in an approved primary source for this specific wine.

Glycemic load per serving is 0.0. 11. Input food_group was 'Grains and staples' which is incorrect for a wine; corrected to 'Beverages'. 12. is_vegan set to false as many wines use animal-derived fining agents (e.g. egg whites, isinglass, casein); specific fining agent information for Villa Medoro not confirmed. 13. Multilingual local names are not applicable for this Italian wine product; no Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese mandatory language requirements apply to Italian cuisine under the Veritas multilingual rules.
